query: How can I configure environment variables after creating a newly created database?
answer:
[Source: database.txt | CHUNKID: 1] 
You can configure environment variables after creating a newly created database by managing several environment variables such as host, database, username, password, etc. Alternatively, you can use a single database "URL" that contains all of the connection information for the database in a single string, which follows a standard schema convention: driver://username:password@host:port/database?options. If the url (or corresponding DATABASE_URL environment variable) configuration option is present, it will be used to extract the database connection and credential information.

query: Which command is used to generate migrations?
answer: The command used to generate migrations is: php artisan make:migration [FILE: migrations.txt | CHUNKID: 0] and [FILE: migrations.txt | CHUNKID: 1].

To use seeders, you can run the db:seed Artisan command, which by default runs the DatabaseSeeder class. This class can be used to call other seed classes using the call method. [seeder.txt | CHUNKID:2] 

You can also use the --class option to specify a specific seeder class to run individually, for example: 
php artisan db:seed --class=UserSeeder [seeder.txt | CHUNKID:2]

Additionally, you can seed your database using the migrate:fresh command, which will drop all tables and re-run all of your migrations: 
php artisan migrate:fresh --seed [seeder.txt | CHUNKID:2]

If you want to force the seeders to run without a prompt, you can use the --force flag: 
php artisan db:seed --force [seeder.txt | CHUNKID:2] 

To generate a seeder, you can execute the make:seeder Artisan command: 
php artisan make:seeder UserSeeder [seeder.txt | CHUNKID:0] 

Within the run method of the seeder class, you can insert data into your database using the query builder or Eloquent model factories. [seeder.txt | CHUNKID:0]

query: why is there no need to clean strings being passed as bindings?
answer:
The Laravel query builder uses PDO parameter binding to protect your application against SQL injection attacks. Therefore, there is no need to clean strings being passed as bindings. 
[Source: queries.txt | Chunk Id: 0]
